meta_title: For Students: freelance pitching to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to freelance pitching and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Freelance Pitching to Solve No SaaS Knowledge
content.seo_description: Overcome the lack of SaaS experience by mastering freelance pitching. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your career.
content.answer_block: Overcome the 'no-experience' barrier by leveraging AI-assisted development to build high-value micro-tools for local businesses. By mastering freelance pitching—positioning yourself as a technical partner rather than a junior coder—you transform your lack of a formal SaaS track record into a competitive edge through speed, transparency, and modern AI-driven delivery.
content.intro:
You stare at the blank terminal, the weight of 'no experience' feeling like a concrete wall. The industry demands a seasoned SaaS veteran, but you are a student with a laptop and a vision. The gap between your current skills and a deployable product feels insurmountable, leaving you trapped in tutorial hell while the market passes you by.

But here is the secret: the market does not care about your resume; it cares about solved problems. By combining the rapid architectural scaffolding of Antigravity with the autonomous coding power of Claude Code, you can bridge the gap from student to freelance developer in a single sprint. Stop waiting for permission to build—start pitching solutions that move the needle for real clients today.

steps.title: Identify a Micro-SaaS Niche
steps.description: Find a local business with a manual process (e.g., appointment scheduling or data entry) that you can automate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Analyze the workflow of a small local coffee shop and suggest three high-impact, low-complexity SaaS features that would increase their daily revenue.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Research local business pain points and propose 3 MVP features'

steps.title: Generate the Technical Architecture
steps.description: Use Antigravity to define the stack. Keep it simple: Next.js, Tailwind, and Supabase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a project architecture for a Next.js 14 app with Supabase auth and Stripe integration. Provide the folder structure and setup commands.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize project with recommended folder structure and dependencies'

steps.title: Build the Core Value Prop
steps.description: Focus on the 'Hero Feature' that solves the client's biggest pain point first.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a clean, responsive React component for a booking dashboard that connects to a Supabase table. Use Tailwind for professional styling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create BookingDashboard component with Supabase CRUD operations'

steps.title: Draft the Pitch Deck
steps.description: Translate your technical work into a business case. Clients buy results, not code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a cold email to a local business owner explaining the prototype I just built. Focus on ROI and time-saved, not the tech stack.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Draft a high-converting cold email template based on the current MVP functionality'

steps.title: Deploy and Iterate
steps.description: Push to Vercel and present the live link. Feedback is your most important development tool.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment checklist for a Next.js app to Vercel, including environment variable setup for Supabase.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Execute build and provide Vercel deployment verification steps'

pro_tips: Never sell code; sell the 'time-to-value' metric. Clients don't care how you built it, only how much time they save.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests before you write your features; it builds immense trust with skeptical clients.
Always include a 'Maintenance Tier' in your pitch to ensure recurring revenue from day one.
If a client asks about your experience, pivot to your current project's performance metrics, not your years in the industry.
Keep your initial MVP scope to under 48 hours of development time to maintain high velocity and low burnout.
content.next_steps: Once your first client accepts the pitch, focus on gathering testimonials. Use those testimonials to raise your rates for the next project, moving from 'student' to 'independent consultant' within 90 days.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework, the tools, and the strategy to bypass the traditional 'years of experience' gatekeeping. Stop planning and start shipping. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
